ksa_text: Knowledge of AI model lifecycle governance, including model versioning strategies, performance monitoring, drift detection, retraining decision logic, and decommissioning procedures.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of performance tuning tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Data Governance is a primary or secondary domain for the VP Data Architecture. This knowledge KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (vp).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Data Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.489). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the VP Data Architecture versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: DAMA-DMBOK, EDM Council, and enterprise CDO job descriptions universally include data governance competencies for data leadership roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Data Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for VP Data Architecture.

decision_rationale: Data Governance knowledge KSA is within scope for the VP Data Architecture at the vp tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
